---
title: "Set up a git token for authentication and refreshing"
author: "Clinton"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Setting up a git token for a repo
- On your profile, select your organizations
- Click on Developer settings, then personal access tokens and the fine-grained tokens
- Click on generate new token and select owner (should be opml-data unless it's for your personal use)
- Configure the right level of access,preferably a given repository and then the respective permissions: mostly metadata, contents and commit status.

## Refreshing the GitHub token
- Follow above steps and click on regenerate button.
- Navigate to the shiny-server folder on the server and run the following commands:  

```{bash}

git remote set-url origin https://your_username:your_personal_access_token@github.com/your_username/your_repo.git

git pull

```
